title: Iseyon Analytics & Strategy: Strengthening Leadership Through Shared Insight in the Heart of Detroit
shortDescription: Iseyon Analytics & Strategy: Strengthening Leadership Through Shared Insight in the Heart of Detroit
description:
**Iseyon Analytics** recently partnered with **Strategy** to co-host a networking event at **Carhartt's flagship location in Detroit, Michigan**, on June 26, 2025, bringing together a select group of business leaders and prospective customers for an evening focused on insight-driven conversation and strategic connection. The gathering provided a productive environment for discussing current organisational challenges and the evolving role of AI and analytics in guiding business decision-making. Carhartt's Detroit space offers a setting that aligns naturally with themes of durability, precision, and purposeful design — qualities that reflect the way many organisations aspire to operate. Within this environment, attendees engaged in thoughtful discussions about the increasing importance of operational clarity, the value of reliable analytics, and the need for strategic alignment in a rapidly changing business landscape. For Iseyon Analytics, the Detroit event serves as a reaffirmation of our commitment to helping businesses transform complexity into clarity. By facilitating conversations that connect real-world challenges with data-driven solutions, we continue to support organisations in making informed, strategic decisions that deliver measurable impact. As a small way to celebrate our partnership and improve the experience, each attendee received a $300 gift voucher to use in-store. We hope this added perk made the evening even more memorable. ## Why Data-Driven Leadership Matters The conversations at the Detroit event highlighted themes that resonate across industries. Leaders increasingly recognise that excellence in execution requires more than intuition — it demands reliable data, coherent analytics strategy, and cross-functional alignment. According to [McKinsey & Company (2025)](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-data-driven-enterprise-of-2025), organisations that commit to data and analytics as strategic assets consistently outperform peers on revenue growth, customer retention, and profitability.

"Data-driven organisations are 23 times more likely to acquire customers, six times as likely to retain them, and 19 times as likely to be profitable."

— McKinsey Global Institute, The Data-Driven Enterprise of 2025
## Analytics Adoption in Leadership & Strategy: 2024–2026 Benchmarks The following industry benchmarks, compiled from leading analyst research, contextualise the themes discussed at the Detroit event.
Enterprise analytics adoption and ROI benchmarks (2024–2026)
MetricFindingSource
CEOs who view data as a critical strategic asset87%PwC Global CEO Survey, 2024
Enterprises reporting measurable ROI from analytics91%Stanford HAI AI Index, 2024
Fortune 500 companies with a Chief Data Officer70%+Gartner Research, 2024
Productivity improvement from data-driven decisions5–6%Harvard Business Review
Reduction in decision cycle time with analytics toolsUp to 40%McKinsey & Company, 2024
*Methodology: All figures sourced from publicly available analyst reports and peer-reviewed research (2023–2026).* ## About Iseyon Analytics and Strategy [Iseyon Analytics](https://www.iseyon.com) is an AI-powered BI consulting firm delivering data transformation services across finance, retail, healthcare, and manufacturing. The company's partnership with **Strategy** reflects a shared commitment to equipping business leaders with the analytics capabilities needed to compete effectively in data-driven markets. Events like the Detroit gathering are part of Iseyon Analytics' broader mission: making advanced analytics accessible to organisations of all sizes and fostering communities of practice around data-driven leadership. --- **References:** - McKinsey Global Institute. (2025). [The Data-Driven Enterprise of 2025](https://www.mckinsey.com/capabilities/quantumblack/our-insights/the-data-driven-enterprise-of-2025). - PwC. (2024). [27th Annual Global CEO Survey](https://www.pwc.com/gx/en/ceo-agenda/ceosurvey.html). - Stanford HAI. (2024). [AI Index Report](https://aiindex.stanford.edu/report/). - Harvard Business Review. (2012). [Big Data: The Management Revolution](https://hbr.org/2012/10/big-data-the-management-revolution).
